# Changelog

All notable changes to Pelorus are documented here. The format is
[Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/); versioning is
[SemVer](https://semver.org/). The `[Unreleased]` block is **rendered** from
`changelog.d/` fragments by `scripts/release/concat-changelog-fragments.sh
--write` — do not edit it by hand (see [changelog.d/README.md](changelog.d/README.md)).

<!-- BEGIN UNRELEASED (generated by scripts/release/concat-changelog-fragments.sh) -->
## [Unreleased]

### Added

- **`libpelorus`** (`libpelorus/`): the shared core library — the Pelorus⇄vmafx side-data interop ABI (`pelorus/interop.h`, `pelorus/interop.c`) and the smart-deband parameter contract (`pelorus/deband.h`). The `PelorusSideData` blob is a versioned, UUID-keyed, append-only AVFrame side-data format both `vf_pelorus_*` and vmafx's `vf_libvmaf*` read/write. Ships a shared conformance fixture (`libpelorus/test/interop_test.c`). ADR-0103, ADR-0105.
- **Docs**: `docs/api/interop-abi.md` (the ABI + C API), `docs/architecture/overview.md` (the two interop seams).
- **`vf_pelorus_deband_vulkan`** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vf_pelorus_deband_vulkan.c`): the flagship Vulkan compute smart-deband filter — f3kdb-style flat-test + TPDF/blue-noise grain + a local-variance detail-protection mask, zero-copy in VRAM as a pre-encode pass. AVOptions: `range`, `thry/thrc`, `grainy/grainc`, `softness`, `detail`, `sample`, `blur`, `dither`, `dynamic`, `protect`, `planes`, `meta`. With `meta=1` it attaches a Pelorus interop blob. Shipped as `ffmpeg-patches/0001-add-vf_pelorus_deband_vulkan.patch` against n8.1.1. ADR-0102, ADR-0104.
- **Reference shader** (`libpelorus/shaders/pelorus_deband.comp`): standalone f3kdb deband, CI-compiled to SPIR-V.
- **Docs**: `docs/metrics/deband.md` (filter + options + VMAF tuning), `docs/backends/vulkan.md`, `docs/usage/ffmpeg.md`, `docs/research/0101-smart-deband.md`.
- **`vf_pelorus_analyze_vulkan`** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vf_pelorus_analyze_vulkan.c`): a pass-through Vulkan compute analyzer that reduces each frame's luma to per-frame banding / variance / edge statistics on the GPU (per-tile shared-memory reduction → sliced SSBO), reads them back (vf_scdet_vulkan pattern), and attaches the *measured* `PEL_SEC_VARIANCE` + `PEL_SEC_BANDING` interop sections — the data the deband filter only approximates. Codec-agnostic. AVOption: `flat`. Shipped as `ffmpeg-patches/0002-add-vf_pelorus_analyze_vulkan.patch` (cumulative on 0001, applies via `git am --3way`). ADR-0109.
- **Reference shader** (`libpelorus/shaders/pelorus_analyze.comp`): standalone reduction, CI-compiled to SPIR-V.
- **Docs**: `docs/metrics/analyze.md`.
- **Control-plane contract** (`docs/api/control-plane.md`): froze the
  autotune-relevant `vf_pelorus_deband_vulkan` AVOptions (`range`, `thry`,
  `thrc`, `grainy`, `grainc`, `softness`, `detail`, `dither`, `dynamic`,
  `protect`) as a stable, versioned contract that vmafx's `vmaf-tune` autotune
  hard-codes against. Renaming/removing/narrowing any frozen knob is a breaking
  change requiring a coordinated two-repo PR. ADR-0110 (builds on ADR-0106).
- **Benchmark harness** (`scripts/bench/`): a pinned, repeatable BD-rate proof that a Pelorus pre-encode pass beats a bare hardware encode. `corpus.lock` (sha256-verified clips) + `fetch-corpus.sh` materialise raw YUV; `run-bench.py` runs the RD ladder and reports BD-rate (`bd_rate.py`, Bjøntegaard) + a CAMBI banding delta. A `--clean-reference` flag scores both arms against the clean ground truth (not the impaired encoder input), and `--vmaf-timeout` reaps the vmaf process that hangs after flushing its JSON. With a temporal-denoise stand-in this shows **−42.94% BD-rate** on high-motion BBB and **−88.94%** on a locked-off scene (both + seeded grain). ADR-0111; [docs/development/benchmarking.md](../../docs/development/benchmarking.md), [bench-results.md](../../docs/development/bench-results.md).
- **`vf_pelorus_denoise_vulkan`** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vf_pelorus_denoise_vulkan.c`): an edge-preserving spatio-temporal denoise filter — the biggest BD-rate pre-encode lever. Single Vulkan compute pass over a causal window (current + *N* previous frames in VRAM, no motion compensation): an NLM-lite joint bilateral (3×3-patch-SSD range weight that refuses to average across edges) blended with per-pixel-gated temporal averaging (a delta above `tcut` breaks the walk so motion can't ghost). AVOptions: `sigma`, `sigmac`, `sigmat`, `strength`, `strengthc`, `blend`, `tdecay`, `tcut`, `patch`, `prev`, `protect`, `planes`, `meta`. With `meta=1` it emits the pre-reserved `PEL_SEC_DENOISE` interop section (residual energy, applied strength, noise-σ estimate, PSNR-vs-input). Host-side param contract `pelorus/denoise.h` (`pel_denoise_params_default`/`_validate`); reference shader `libpelorus/shaders/pelorus_denoise.comp` kept in lockstep. Shipped as `ffmpeg-patches/0003-add-vf_pelorus_denoise_vulkan.patch`. ADR-0112, ADR-0111.
- **Param contract** (`libpelorus/include/pelorus/denoise.h`, `libpelorus/src/denoise_params.c`): `PelorusDenoiseParams` + defaults/validation, the vmafx-autotune-facing view of the denoise knobs. ADR-0112.
- **`vf_pelorus_analyze` auto-ROI** (`roi=1`): auto-detect banding-prone tiles (per-32×32 GPU variance reduction — a tile bands when its variance is above a constant floor yet below the texture threshold, i.e. a smooth ramp) and emit `AV_FRAME_DATA_REGIONS_OF_INTEREST` with a negative `qoffset`, which `libx265`/`libx264`/QSV/VAAPI honor with **no patch**. Proven to beat x265's built-in `aq-mode 2`: **−47% banding (CAMBI 1.355→0.711) at iso-bitrate and iso-VMAF** on gradient-over-detail content — variance-AQ starves the flat gradient it can't see; the banding-ROI rescues it automatically. ADR-0114 (Tier 0). Options: `roi`, `flat`, `roi_strength`, `grad_lo`.
- **Roadmap ADRs** for the motion + encoder-steering work: **ADR-0113** (`vf_pelorus_mc` — GPU block-matching motion estimation feeding MC-temporal denoise by *warping* the fetch, the verified fix for the in-filter MC dead-end; plus a gated, NVENC-only external-ME-hint path) and **ADR-0114** (encoder-steering strategy — feed Pelorus's GPU-measured maps to every encoder: `AV_FRAME_DATA_REGIONS_OF_INTEREST` works *today* on libx264/libx265/libvpx/QSV/VAAPI; dense per-block delta-QP via our NVENC `qpDeltaMap` / QSV `mfxExtMBQP` patches; the cross-vendor `VK_KHR_video_encode_quantization_map` zero-copy path). Both record the honest caveat that ROI/QP steering is bit-*redistribution* (perceptual win, must beat the encoder's built-in AQ, magnitudes unverified until measured per ADR-0111).
- **NVENC ROI steering** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/nvenc-pelorus-roi.patch`, `-pelorus_roi 1`): stock `nvenc.c` ignores `AV_FRAME_DATA_REGIONS_OF_INTEREST` (unlike libx264/libx265/QSV/VAAPI); this patch rasterizes it into NVENC's per-block `qpDeltaMap` (codec-dependent grid; libx264 qoffset→QP convention; reviving the dormant `NVENC_HAVE_QP_MAP_MODE` guard). So the proven `vf_pelorus_analyze roi=1` banding map now steers **NVIDIA-hardware** bit allocation — which stock ffmpeg cannot. Proven: **−41% banding, +0.10 VMAF, +3% bitrate** (`hevc_nvenc -rc constqp`, ADR-0114 Tier 1, bench-results.md v0.5). `generate.sh` extended to carry this libavcodec edit as patch 0004. ADR-0114.
- **QSV ROI steering** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/qsv-pelorus-roi.patch`, `-pelorus_roi 1`): stock `hevc_qsv`/`h264_qsv` map `AV_FRAME_DATA_REGIONS_OF_INTEREST` only onto coarse `mfxExtEncoderROI` rectangles (capped at 256, rectangle granularity); this patch instead rasterizes the same side data into Intel QSV's *dense* per-block delta-QP map (`mfxExtMBQP`, `MFX_MBQP_MODE_QP_DELTA`, attached per frame; `mfxExtCodingOption3::EnableMBQP` enabled at init), and supersedes the rectangle path while active (the two are mutually exclusive per the oneVPL spec). So the proven `vf_pelorus_analyze roi=1` banding map now steers **Intel-hardware** bit allocation at full per-cell fidelity — mirroring the merged NVENC `qpDeltaMap` patch (ADR-0114 Tier 1). Portable by construction: 16×16 block size is the SDK-documented MBQP alignment for AVC and HEVC (not a GPU-specific coding-tree size); `EnableMBQP` is runtime-capability-probed (CQP rate control only — non-CQP warns once and passes through) and compile-gated by `QSV_HAVE_MBQP` (oneVPL/MSDK ≥ 1.13); no device-node/driver/entrypoint assumptions; zero behaviour change when off (default). `generate.sh` extended to carry this libavcodec edit as patch 0005. Verified: `cc -fsyntax-only` of the changed TUs + clean full-stack regeneration; on-Intel-HW BD-rate proof is a follow-up (no numbers fabricated). ADR-0114.
- **On-GPU quantization-map raster** for the Tier-2 "via Vulkan" QP-map steering (`libpelorus/shaders/pelorus_qpmap.comp` + `ffmpeg-patches/files/vulkan-pelorus-qpmap.patch`, patch 0009): the `VK_KHR_video_encode_quantization_map` texel image is now filled by a compute dispatch that reads the coalesced ROI rectangle list from a small SSBO and `imageStore`s the per-texel delta-QP (`R8_SINT`) or emphasis (`R8_UNORM`) directly, replacing the host per-texel raster + staging-buffer upload the v0.1.0 path used (the follow-up the Tier-2 patch itself flagged). The dispatch records on the encode command buffer and is preferred only when the encode queue family also advertises `VK_QUEUE_COMPUTE_BIT` (no cross-queue ownership transfer); otherwise it transparently falls back to the host raster path. Both share the same `qoffset`→ΔQP convention; capability-probed, default off, no device/box assumptions. The standalone `pelorus_qpmap.comp` and the patch's inline GLSL stay in lockstep (AGENTS hard rule 4) and the reference shader compiles in the fast gate (glslang). **Compile-verified only**: `vulkan_encode.c` + the three codec TUs build clean against FFmpeg n8.1.1 with the system Vulkan-video headers, the inline GLSL compiles under glslang, and the full 0001–0010 stack replays onto pristine n8.1.1; runtime on-HW proof remains blocked on the dev box's missing encode-feedback flags (a driver gap, not a patch defect). No BD-rate gain claimed. ADR-0114 Tier 2.
- **Cross-vendor "via Vulkan" QP-map steering** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vulkan-pelorus-qpmap.patch`, `-pelorus_roi 1`): patches `libavcodec/vulkan_encode.c` to drive `VK_KHR_video_encode_quantization_map`, so the proven `vf_pelorus_analyze roi=1` banding map (the standard `AV_FRAME_DATA_REGIONS_OF_INTEREST` side data) steers bit allocation on **every Vulkan-encode-capable GPU** — `h264_vulkan`/`hevc_vulkan`/`av1_vulkan` — with no host roundtrip (ADR-0114 Tier 2, the cross-vendor endgame). A single `-pelorus_roi` AVOption in the shared `VULKAN_ENCODE_COMMON_OPTIONS` macro exposes it on all three codecs. The map kind is driver-probed from the negotiated rate control: a signed **delta-QP map** (`R8_SINT`) under CQP, or an **emphasis map** (`R8_UNORM`) under CBR/VBR. Everything runtime-probes the extension + capability flag + texel size and degrades to a one-shot warning + pass-through; a frame with no ROI binds no map (zero behaviour change); the path is compile-gated by `VK_KHR_video_encode_quantization_map`. `generate.sh` extended to carry this libavcodec edit as patch 0008. **Compile-verified only** (`vulkan_encode.c` builds clean under FFmpeg's `-std=c17 -Wall` set; full stack replays onto pristine n8.1.1); on-HW proof is blocked on the dev box by a driver missing the encode-feedback flags (`BUFFER_OFFSET`/`BYTES_WRITTEN`) — a driver limitation, not a patch defect. No BD-rate gain claimed. ADR-0114.
- **`vf_pelorus_grain_estimate_vulkan`** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vf_pelorus_grain_estimate_vulkan.c`): a film-grain-synthesis (FGS) parameter **estimator** — the dual of the denoise lever. A pass-through Vulkan compute pass (modelled on `vf_pelorus_analyze_vulkan`) measures, per luma intensity band, the high-frequency residual variance (luma minus a 3×3 box low-pass) over locally-flat neighbourhoods (an edge gate excludes structure), plus a global lag-1 spatial-correlation accumulator as a coarse AR seed. The per-band RMS residual maps to the AV1 AOM piecewise scaling function `y_points[value, scaling]`; the lag-1 proxy seeds `ar_coeffs_y`. AVOptions: `edge`, `strength`, `model` (`aom`/`h274`), `native`. The filter emits BOTH the pre-reserved `PEL_SEC_FILMGRAIN` interop section (codec-neutral intent + AV1 params + H.274 mode scalars + `grain_model` tag; no ABI bump) AND a native `AV_FRAME_DATA_FILM_GRAIN_PARAMS` (`AV_FILM_GRAIN_PARAMS_AV1`) so a downstream FFmpeg AV1 encoder honours the estimate with no Pelorus-specific BSF. Reference shader `libpelorus/shaders/pelorus_grain_estimate.comp` kept in lockstep. The AV1-OBU/H.274-SEI bitstream filter, the full per-lag AR fit, and explicit chroma estimation are documented follow-ups. Shipped as `ffmpeg-patches/0006-add-vf_pelorus_grain_estimate_vulkan.patch`. ADR-0115.
- **NVENC external ME hints** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/nvenc-pelorus-me-hints.patch`, `-pelorus_me_hints 1`, H.264/HEVC): the gated Tier-3 consumer of the `vf_pelorus_mc_vulkan` producer (ADR-0116). Stock `nvenc.c` always runs the ASIC's own motion search and cannot be seeded; this patch reads the per-block MV field the producer emits as the `PEL_SEC_MOTION` interop section and feeds it to NVENC's external-ME-hint input (`enableExternalMEHints` + `maxMEHintCountsPerBlock` at init, per-frame `NV_ENC_PIC_PARAMS::meExternalHints` + `meHintCountsPerBlock`). One L0 candidate per 16×16 block (the SDK external-hint granularity for AVC/HEVC); the producer's `bsize` grid is resampled onto the 16×16 grid by center-block sampling; vectors are clamped to NVENC's hint bitfields (mvx S12, mvy S10). Value is **encode SPEED, not quality** — the ASIC seeds/short-circuits its own search. Default OFF (zero behaviour change); compile-gated by a new `NVENC_HAVE_EXTERNAL_ME_HINTS` macro (SDK 8.1+); capability runtime-probed (`NV_ENC_CAPS_SUPPORT_MEONLY_MODE`) with graceful degrade + one-shot warn; AV1 skipped (different per-superblock hint struct); the blob is parsed inline (no libavcodec→libpelorus link). **No speedup number ships** — the MV field is wired into NVENC's external-ME input and verified to compile against the ffnvcodec headers and replay onto pristine n8.1.1; the on-hardware encode-speed A/B is a documented follow-up. `generate.sh` extended to carry this libavcodec edit as patch 0008. ADR-0116, ADR-0114 Tier 3.
- **`vf_pelorus_mc_vulkan`** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vf_pelorus_mc_vulkan.c`): a GPU block-matching **motion estimator** that produces a per-block integer-pel motion-vector field for the current frame relative to the previous frame and attaches it as the pre-reserved `PEL_SEC_MOTION` interop section (frame scalars — global motion, magnitude mean/p95, entropy, scene-cut flag — plus the dense `int16 (dx,dy)` per-block grid). The frame passes through unchanged (the analyzer shape). One workgroup per block: cooperative SAD reduced in shared memory, then a predictor-seeded small-diamond descent transcribed from FFmpeg's `libavfilter/motion_estimation.c` (`ff_me_search_epzs`/`_ds`); predictors are the zero MV, the previous frame's global-motion MV, and the collocated previous-frame block MV (a persistent MV SSBO ping-ponged frame to frame). Value is **encode speed, not quality** — the MV field is a hint for an encoder's external motion search (e.g. NVENC `NV_ENC_EXTERNAL_ME_HINT`); the NVENC ME-hint consumer is a gated follow-up (ADR-0114 Tier 3), and it deliberately does **not** feed the denoiser (raw-pixel block-match is noise-limited, ADR-0113). AVOptions: `bsize` (default 16), `search` (default 24), `meta` (default on). No interop ABI bump — `PEL_SEC_MOTION` was reserved at ABI 1.0. Reference shader `libpelorus/shaders/pelorus_mc.comp` kept in lockstep. Shipped as `ffmpeg-patches/0007-add-vf_pelorus_mc_vulkan.patch`. ADR-0116, ADR-0113, ADR-0114.
- **`pelorus_fgs` bitstream filter** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/h265_pelorus_fgs_bsf.c` → `libavcodec/bsf/pelorus_fgs.c`): the HEVC/H.274 leg of the film-grain round-trip the grain estimator (ADR-0115) started. Inserts an ITU-T H.274 Film Grain Characteristics (FGC) SEI message (HEVC payload type 19) as a prefix NAL into each HEVC access unit, so a conforming decoder re-synthesizes grain that was removed before encode (e.g. by `vf_pelorus_denoise_vulkan`). Stock FFmpeg HEVC encoders do not carry film-grain frame side data into the bitstream — the AV1 leg already round-trips through the native `AV_FRAME_DATA_FILM_GRAIN_PARAMS` channel, but HEVC had no automatic path — so this BSF carries the parameters into the elementary stream itself. Built on the coded-bitstream (CBS) generic BSF framework (`cbs_h265`), exactly like `hevc_metadata`: each access unit is parsed, one FGC SEI is added via `ff_cbs_sei_add_message()`, and CBS reassembles the unit. **Honest scope**: this is the static-model leg — the H.274 model is supplied via AVOptions (`model_id`, `blending_mode`, `log2_scale`, `components`, `intensity_low`/`high`, `scale_y`, `scale_c`, `persistence`, `skip_existing`), the canonical FFmpeg metadata-BSF contract; a BSF works on `AVPacket`s and no stock encoder forwards the estimator's per-frame grain side data onto the coded packet, so per-frame estimate→SEI plumbing through an arbitrary HEVC encoder is not expressible in n8.1.1. The producer's H.274 scalars (`h274_model_id`/`h274_blending_mode`/`h274_log2_scale`) and per-band RMS scaling map directly onto these options (mapping recipe in `docs/usage/grain-fgs-bsf.md`). Opt-in and no-op by construction (passes through unless a component is selected; `skip_existing` avoids double-stamping); does not link `libpelorus`. Verified end to end: SEI inserted into a libx265 stream and parsed back with `trace_headers` showing the exact configured values. Shipped as `ffmpeg-patches/0008-add-pelorus_fgs_bsf.patch`. ADR-0117, ADR-0115.
- **NVENC hardware AV1 film grain** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/nvenc-pelorus-film-grain.patch`, `-pelorus_film_grain 1`, `av1_nvenc` only): the hardware-AV1 leg of the FGS round-trip (ADR-0118). Stock `av1_nvenc` never enables NVENC's AV1 film-grain synthesis, so the estimate `vf_pelorus_grain_estimate_vulkan` (ADR-0115) emits is dropped and the grain is coded as residual. This patch carries that estimate into NVENC's AV1 film-grain config: at init it sets `NV_ENC_CONFIG_AV1::enableFilmGrainParams` and points `filmGrainParams` at a persistent `NV_ENC_FILM_GRAIN_PARAMS_AV1`; per frame it refills that struct from the frame's grain params (the native `AV_FRAME_DATA_FILM_GRAIN_PARAMS`/`AV_FILM_GRAIN_PARAMS_AV1` channel preferred, the `PEL_SEC_FILMGRAIN` interop section parsed inline as fallback) and raises the AV1 pic-params `filmGrainParamsUpdate` flag (time-varying model). The `AVFilmGrainAOMParams` set maps field-for-field onto the NVENC struct (numbers shifted to NVENC's "minus N"/"plus 128" conventions, point and AR-coefficient arrays copied element-wise, all writes clamped). With the H.274 BSF (patch 0010) for HEVC and native side data for AV1 software encoders, this completes the FGS round-trip on NVIDIA hardware AV1. Default OFF (zero behaviour change); registered on `av1_nvenc` only (H.264/HEVC NVENC have no AV1 film grain); compile-gated by a new `NVENC_HAVE_AV1_FILM_GRAIN` macro (SDK 12.0+) with a one-shot warn + graceful degrade; the blob is parsed inline (no libavcodec→libpelorus link). **No grain-match or BD-rate number ships** — the estimate is wired into NVENC's AV1 film-grain config and verified to compile against the ffnvcodec 13.0 headers, register on `av1_nvenc` only, and replay onto pristine n8.1.1 (the `ffmpeg` binary built and `-h encoder=av1_nvenc` shows the option, absent from `hevc_nvenc`/`h264_nvenc`); the on-hardware visual/BD-rate A/B is a documented follow-up. `generate.sh` extended to carry this libavcodec edit as patch 0011. ADR-0118, ADR-0115, ADR-0114.
- **Closed-loop QP feedback** (interop ABI 1.0 → **1.1**): new append-only section `PEL_SEC_QPREPORT` (bit `1u << 5`, `PelorusQpReportSection`) carries the encoder's *actual* post-encode honored QP/bit decisions back into the Pelorus side-data blob — the read-back half of encoder steering (ADR-0114 step 6 / ADR-0119). It holds frame-mean QP, PSNR Y/U/V, total bits, intra/inter/skipped block counts, per-cell actual-QP + bits map offsets, a `honored_fraction` verification scalar, and a `report_source` tag (`enum pel_qp_report_source`: QSV today; NVENC/Vulkan reserved). Fed by a vendor-neutral reader stub `pel_qp_report_from_blocks` that folds an abstract per-block QP grid onto the cell grid, so libpelorus links no encoder SDK (vmafx vendors `interop.c` verbatim). The append-only ABI section + pack/parse + conformance fixture (round-trip, fold, forward-compat) + the reader stub are **working**; the libavcodec QSV `mfxEncodeStats`/`mfxExtEncodedUnitsInfo` extraction and the populated `honored_fraction` are the documented follow-up. No measured honored-fraction or BD-rate number is claimed (no HW encode run). `PELORUS_ABI_MINOR` bumped to 1; conformance test reports ABI 1.1. ADR-0119.
- **libaom-av1 ROI steering** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/libaom-pelorus-roi.patch`, `-pelorus_roi 1`): patches `libavcodec/libaomenc.c` so `libaom-av1` honours `AV_FRAME_DATA_REGIONS_OF_INTEREST` — the standard ROI side data `vf_pelorus_analyze roi=1` emits — via libaom's segment-based `AOME_SET_ROI_MAP` (ADR-0114 Tier 1, the software-AV1 leg next to NVENC/QSV). Unlike the dense per-block delta-QP maps, libaom uses a segment model: ≤ 8 distinct qindex deltas per frame (`AOM_MAX_SEGMENTS`) over a 4×4-luma-cell grid. The bridge quantises each frame's region `qoffset`s into ≤ 8 segments (segment 0 = background), paints the cell grid by nearest segment (first region wins on overlap, like `libx264`), and pushes the map per frame before the encode. A single `pelorus_roi` AVOption (default off) exposes it; a frame with no ROI side data pushes nothing (zero behaviour change). **Verified on hardware** (built into ffmpeg n8.1.1 `--enable-libaom`, ran the analyze→libaom pipeline on a banding clip): no crash, valid output, the bridge builds a sane 120×68 grid and quantises 32 regions into 6 segments. **Honest negative on quality**: stock libaom 3.14.1's `AOME_SET_ROI_MAP` returns `AOM_CODEC_INVALID_PARAM` in FFmpeg's encoder configuration (the control is wired only for the RTC delta-q path upstream), so the map is ignored and CAMBI is identical with the option on or off (0.93193 both). The bridge degrades gracefully (one warning, no bias) and becomes effective for free once a libaom release enables the control on the path FFmpeg uses — an upstream limitation, not a patch defect. `generate.sh` extended to carry this libavcodec edit as patch 0012. See `docs/backends/libaom-roi.md` and ADR-0120.
- **SVT-AV1 ROI steering** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/svtav1-pelorus-roi.patch`, `-pelorus_roi 1` on `libsvtav1` / `av1_svt`): stock `libsvtav1` consumes **no** ROI side data at all — the flagship modern AV1 software encoder had zero Pelorus steering. This patch consumes the same `AV_FRAME_DATA_REGIONS_OF_INTEREST` side data the merged `vf_pelorus_analyze roi=1` emits and maps it onto SVT-AV1's native per-64×64-superblock ROI segment map (`SvtAv1RoiMapEvt`): each frame's regions are rasterized onto the superblock grid and quantised into ≤ 8 AV1 segments, each carrying a `seg_qp` qindex delta (negative = more bits, matching `qoffset`), attached per frame via the `ROI_MAP_EVENT` private-data node with `enable_roi_map` flipped on at init. Per-frame events are heap-owned and freed at close (the library keeps the bare pointer live across its async lookahead). Compile-gated by `SVT_AV1_CHECK_VERSION(1, 6, 0)` (the release that introduced the ROI-map ABI) — older SVT-AV1 warns once and passes through; `enable_roi_map` is RC-mode agnostic but constant-quality (`-crf`/`-qp`) is recommended (the encoder's own AQ can override the map); zero behaviour change when off (default). `generate.sh` + `series.txt` extended to carry this libavcodec edit as patch 0012. **Verified on hardware** (real ffmpeg n8.1.1 + full stack, `--enable-libsvtav1`, SvtAv1Enc 4.1.0, RTX 4090 Vulkan): `analyze roi=1 → libsvtav1 -pelorus_roi 1` runs without crashing, the ROI is honored (bitstream + bitrate change only with the option on; engagement logged), banding drops modestly (CAMBI −1.5 % at CRF 35 / −0.5 % at CRF 45 on a synthetic localized-banding clip — an honest, modest gain, far below the NVENC −41 % on its harder source; a real-content BD-rate run is a follow-up). Film grain (`fgs_table`) deferred — SVT-AV1 exposes only a whole-stream init-time pointer, no per-frame film-grain channel (`FILM_GRAIN_PARAM` is commented out in the public header). ADR-0121.
- **Runnable QP-feedback reader** (closes ADR-0114 step 6 on a box without a working HW encoder): a SDK-free x265 `--csv --csv-log-level 2` stat reader populates the `PEL_SEC_QPREPORT` interop section with the encoder's *actual* post-encode numbers. `pel_x265_csv_parse` locates the `Type/POC/QP/Bits/PSNR` columns by header name and admits only coded-frame rows (dropping x265's trailing blank + `Summary` block); `pel_qp_report_from_x265_frames` folds the GOP into bit-weighted mean honored QP, total bits, bit-weighted PSNR, and a `honored_fraction` = sign-agreement between the requested and the honored per-frame delta-QP. Frame-granular (x265 CSV exposes no per-CTU QP), so `qp_valid` stays 0 and no per-cell map is written. This is **append-only consumer code** — no wire field/section changes, `PELORUS_ABI_MINOR` stays 1.1. A new demonstrator `tools/pelorus_qp_report` runs the full path (parse → fold → `pel_blob_pack` → `pel_blob_find_section` → print); a 17-frame `testsrc2` clip at `--qp 32 --aq-mode 0` yields **measured** `avg_qp ≈ 32.21`, `total_bits = 104744`, `psnr_y/u/v ≈ 38.6/37.6/37.0 dB`, `honored_fraction = 0.1765` (a flat request scores low because x265 spread the QP by slice type). The QSV `mfxEncodeBlkStats` per-block reader stays the HW follow-up (Arc-A low-power-bugged); no BD-rate number is claimed. New build option `tools` (default on). ADR-0122; [docs/metrics/qp-feedback.md](../../docs/metrics/qp-feedback.md), [docs/api/interop-abi.md](../../docs/api/interop-abi.md).
- **`vf_pelorus_dehalo_vulkan`** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vf_pelorus_dehalo_vulkan.c`): anime / 2D **dehalo + dering** — a single-pass zero-copy Vulkan compute port of HAvsFunc `DeHalo_alpha` + `FineDehalo` (`FF_VK_REP_FLOAT` UNORM, bit-depth-agnostic, luma only; chroma passes through). A strong box blur is the halo-free target; a `lowsens`/`highsens` sensitivity mask from the removed local contrast drives a remove-only asymmetric pull toward the blur (`darkstr` for dark halos, `brightstr` for bright); a `FineDehalo` Sobel edge mask dilated by `ring` confines the pull to the halo band next to line-art so the drawing and open gradients are protected. AVOptions: `blur`, `darkstr`, `brightstr`, `lowsens`, `highsens`, `edge`, `ring`, `planes`. A pure pixel transform — it does not link libpelorus and emits no interop side data. Foundation of the planned `tune=anime` pipeline. Reference shader `libpelorus/shaders/pelorus_dehalo.comp` kept in lockstep. Compile-verified and glslang-clean; the on-content tuning of the defaults and the SSIMULACRA2 / edge-region VMAF-NEG / CAMBI proof against a clean anime ground truth are documented follow-ups (no BD-rate/quality number claimed yet). Shipped as `ffmpeg-patches/0012-add-vf_pelorus_dehalo_vulkan.patch`. (ADR-0123)
- **`vf_pelorus_aa_vulkan`** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vf_pelorus_aa_vulkan.c`): anime warp anti-aliasing + optional line-darkening — a single-pass, zero-copy Vulkan compute (`FF_VK_REP_FLOAT` UNORM, bit-depth-agnostic) port of awarpsharp2 + FastLineDarken, and the second stage of the anime `tune` chain after `vf_pelorus_dehalo_vulkan` (ADR-0123). It builds a blurred Sobel edge-strength map and *warps* each luma pixel toward the nearest edge by the gradient of that map (bilinear resample at the displaced position), pulling stair-stepped "jaggie" samples onto the line — anti-aliasing + thinning with no sharpen ringing — then optionally deepens the dark side of real edges (Sobel-gated) so lines stay crisp. Luma only; chroma passes through. A pure transform: it emits no side data and does **not** link `libpelorus` (deps-only registration). AVOptions: `blur` (2), `depth` (8.0), `thresh` (0.5), `darkstr` (0.0 = off), `edge` (0.08), `planes` (0x1 = luma). Reference shader `libpelorus/shaders/pelorus_aa.comp` kept in lockstep. Honest caveat: the port is compile-verified + glslang-clean only — the defaults are unproven on content, and the SSIMULACRA2 / edge-region VMAF-NEG proof against clean anime ground truth is the documented follow-up; no quality number claimed. Shipped as patch 0012. (ADR-0124)
- **Anime tune** (docs capstone): a recommended GPU pre-encode pipeline for animation that composes the existing filters — `pelorus_analyze_vulkan roi=1` (auto-detected flat-ROI) + `pelorus_dehalo_vulkan` (ring/halo removal) + `pelorus_aa_vulkan` (warp anti-aliasing + line-darkening) + `pelorus_deband_vulkan` (smart deband) — at 10-bit, then steers the encoder's bits onto the flats with `-pelorus_roi 1` under constant-QP. Each leg targets a distinct anime artefact (banding, starved flats, ringing, jaggies); presented as a documented, retunable chain rather than a new meta-filter (the filters are independent and composable). The per-leg ROI wins are measured (−47%/−41% banding); the composed end-to-end SSIMULACRA2 + CAMBI proof is the documented follow-up. New guide [docs/usage/anime.md](docs/usage/anime.md) (ADR-0125).
- **`vf_pelorus_scenecut`** (`ffmpeg-patches/files/vf_pelorus_scenecut.c`): scene-cut → **forced IDR**, a vendor-neutral encoder-steering consumer that completes the ADR-0114 strategy on the temporal/structural axis. A **metadata-only** filter (`AVFILTER_FLAG_METADATA_ONLY` — no shader, no GPU work, no pixels touched) that reads `PEL_SEC_MOTION.has_scene_cut` (the flag `vf_pelorus_mc_vulkan` already emits, ADR-0116) from the frame's Pelorus side data via `pel_blob_find_section` and, on a cut frame, sets `frame->pict_type = AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I` (+ `AV_FRAME_FLAG_KEY`) so the downstream encoder opens a fresh GOP exactly at the cut instead of predicting across the unrelated picture and re-keying at the next periodic IDR. Codec-agnostic — `pict_type == I` is honoured as a forced keyframe by x264/x265/NVENC/QSV/SVT-AV1 with **no per-encoder patch** (unlike the ROI/delta-QP tiers); links `libpelorus` but is **not** gated on Vulkan. AVOption: `force_idr` (bool, default on; `0` = transparent pass-through). Recommended placement: after `hwdownload`, just before the encoder (the motion side data rides the frame via `av_frame_copy_props`). No interop ABI bump — it *consumes* the pre-reserved `PEL_SEC_MOTION` section. Producer + consumer + mechanism are build-verified; the end-to-end BD-rate proof (an A/B of scene-cut-aligned GOPs vs periodic IDR on real multi-shot content, ADR-0111 methodology) is a documented follow-up — no number claimed yet. (ADR-0126)

### Fixed

- **FFmpeg link of `libpelorus`**: the patch stack ran `require_pkg_config` (which adds cflags but not link libs), so a fully-linked `ffmpeg` failed with `undefined reference to pel_blob_pack` — a `.o`-only CI build never caught it. `generate.sh` now also emits `add_extralibs $libpelorus_extralibs`, and CI builds + links the `ffmpeg` binary and smoke-tests that the filters register. ADR-0104.
- **`bd_rate.py` sign**: it computed `integ(baseline) − integ(pelorus)`, sign-flipping both BD-rate and BD-VMAF (a 50% saving read as ~+75%). Corrected to `integ(pelorus) − integ(baseline)`, with a self-test (half-bitrate ⇒ −50%, identical ⇒ 0%, double ⇒ +100%). ADR-0111.
- **`block-unsafe-bash` hook**: the root-wipe guard matched any `rm -rf /…` path, blocking legitimate `rm -rf /tmp/…`. Narrowed to the filesystem root only.
- **QSV ROI (`-pelorus_roi 1`) SIGSEGV under CQP**: `qsvenc_setup_roi()` re-derived its `QSVEncContext` from `avctx->priv_data`, but for the wrapped `hevc_qsv`/`h264_qsv` encoders that pointer is the wrapper struct (the real context is `&wrapper->qsv`), so the ROI write landed on a misaligned pointer and faulted. Fixed by passing the correct `QSVEncContext` in as a parameter, as every other qsvenc helper does. ADR-0114.
- **`vf_pelorus_grain_estimate_vulkan` SIGSEGV on the first GPU frame**: the inline GLSL `% SLICES` modulo was passed unescaped through an `av_bprintf` format string, so `%S` was interpreted as a wide-string (`%ls`) conversion and dereferenced garbage when the shader was assembled. Escaped to `%%` so the literal `%` reaches the GLSL. ADR-0115.

<!-- END UNRELEASED -->

[Unreleased]: https://github.com/VMAFx/pelorus/commits/master
